samples.xml_description:
<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate 4 input data and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum_value>0</enum_value>
            <enum_value>1</enum_value>
            <enum_value>2</enum_value>
            <enum_value>3</enum_value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic>
            <reg id="accu_reg" width_description="[9:0]">Accumulation register.</reg>
            <reg id="valid_out_reg">Valid output register.</reg>
            <always>
                <posedge><!</posedge></always>
            </sequential_logic>
        </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    <description>
    When valid_in is 1, data_in is a valid input. Accumulate four valid input data_in values and calculate the output data_out by adding these four values together. There is no output when there are fewer than four data_in inputs in the interim. Along with the output data_out, a cycle of valid_out=1 will appear as a signal. The valid_out signal is set to 1 when the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results. Otherwise, it is set to 0.
    </description>
    <if>
        <description>Reset state: When rst_n is 0, reset all registers to 0. Go to state 0.</description>
        <condition><!</condition>
        <true>
            <description>Reset accu_reg to 0.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
            <description>Reset valid_out_reg to 0.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
            <description>Go to state 0.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
        </true>
    </if>
    <if>
        <description>Initial state: When valid_in is 1, accumulate data_in to accu_reg and go to state 1.</description>
        <condition><!</condition>
        <true>
            <description>Accumulate data_in to accu_reg.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
            <description>Go to state 1.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
        </true>
    </if>
    <if>
        <description>State 1: When valid_in is 1, accumulate data_in to accu_reg and go to state 2.</description>
        <condition><!</condition>
        <true>
            <description>Accumulate data_in to accu_reg.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
            <description>Go to state 2.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
        </true>
    </if>
    <if>
        <description>State 2: When valid_in is 1, accumulate data_in to accu_reg and go to state 3.</description>
        <condition><!</condition>
        <true>
            <description>Accumulate data_in to accu_reg.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
            <description>Go to state 3.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
        </true>
    </if>
    <if>
        <description>State 3: When valid_in is 1, accumulate data_in to accu_reg and check if 4 data_in values have been accumulated.</description>
        <condition><!</condition>
        <true>
            <description>Accumulate data_in to accu_reg.</description>
            <assignment><!</assignment>
            <description>Check if 4 data_in values have been accumulated.</description>
            <if>
                <description>If 4 data_in values have been accumulated, output accu_reg to data_out and set valid_out_reg to 1. Go to state 0.</description>
                <condition><!</condition>
                <true>
                    <description>Output accu_reg to data_out.</description>
                    <assignment><!</assignment>
                    <description>Set valid_out_reg to 1.</description>
                    <assignment><!</assignment>
                    <description>Go to state 0.</description>
                    <assignment><!</assignment>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <description>Go to state 1.</description>
                    <assignment><!</assignment>
                </false>
            </if>
        </true>
    </if>
    </implementation>
</module>
